settings.headline: Three steps to life-changing breathing.
settings.lead: Watch the 90-second tutorial, then walk through each step at your own pace. The whole routine takes under a minute once you've got it.

settings.heading: A two-part system. No drugs, nothing inside your nose.
settings.body:
Aira sits on the outside of your nose. A reusable magnetic band connects to two small adhesive tabs placed just above your nostrils — gently widening your airway from the outside.

Your nose returns to normal the moment you take it off.
settings.card1_body: Lasts months. Holds the structural pull.
settings.card2_body: One pair per night. Replace as you go.

settings.description: For narrower nose bridges. Lower-impact wear and sleep.
Most popular for sport & high-impact use. Slightly tighter pull.
Most popular for sleep. Wider opening, longer wear.
For wider noses. Maximum airflow at rest.

settings.heading: Find your size.
settings.lead: The right band rests with its tips on the outer edges of your nostrils — without pinching them or pressing them shut. Every Aira Kit ships with four sizes so you can find your fit on night one.
settings.visual_label: Sizing photo — band on nose, callouts pointing to nostril edges
settings.tip_body:
Sleep — start a size up for wider airflow.

Sport — size down so the tabs hold under sweat & movement.
settings.cta_title: Get all four sizes in the Aira Kit.
settings.cta_body: Try every band on night one — keep the one that fits.

settings.title: Clean
settings.image_label: Clean — soap & water on the bridge
settings.body: Use a gentle cleanser to remove makeup, oils, and dirt from the bridge of your nose.

settings.title: Dry
settings.body: Pat — don't rub. Even a little moisture stops adhesive from settling properly.

settings.title: Skip the lotion
settings.image_label: Skip moisturiser & oils on the bridge
settings.body: Moisturisers, serums, and oils are the #1 reason tabs lift early. Apply Aira first, skincare around it.

settings.heading: Prep your skin.
settings.lead: Adhesive sticks to clean, dry skin. Twenty seconds of prep is the difference between tabs that hold for a full night and ones that wander off at 4am.
settings.tip_body: Run a 24-hour patch test with a single tab before your first full night. Discontinue if you notice irritation.

settings.title: A · Peel daintily
settings.image_label: A · Peel a tab daintily by the edge
settings.body: Pinch the tab by its outer edge. The less you touch the adhesive, the longer it holds.

settings.title: B · Place as shown
settings.image_label: B · Place tab just above each nostril
settings.body: One tab on each side of your nose, just above the nostril — steel disc facing outward.

settings.title: C · Shark fins up
settings.image_label: C · Apply band, magnetic ends up
settings.body: Bring the band in and let the magnets click. The fin tips face the sky — never down.

settings.heading: Apply tabs & band.
settings.lead: This is the part everyone gets wrong on the first try. Take it slow — the band needs to sit symmetrically, with the magnetic ends pointing up.
settings.callout_body: Press each tab for 10–15 seconds with the warmth of your finger. The adhesive activates with heat and pressure — this single move adds hours of wear.

settings.title: Place tabs on the bridge
settings.image_label: Tab placed too high — on bridge
settings.body: Too high and the band can't pull outward. Tabs go at the soft fleshy part — just above each nostril.

settings.title: Place above the nostril
settings.image_label: Tab placed correctly — just above nostril
settings.body: On the soft tissue where the nostril meets the cheek — that's where the pull does its work.

settings.title: Wear the band upside down
settings.body: If the magnetic ends point down, the band's curve fights you. The fins should face the sky.

settings.title: Shark fins up
settings.body: Magnetic ends pointing up. The band arcs above your nostrils like a smile, not a frown.

settings.heading: Get it right the first time.
settings.lead: A few small things make the difference between Aira working brilliantly and slipping by 3am.
settings.cta_title: See how others got it right.
settings.cta_body: 9,000+ verified reviews from Aira wearers around the world.

settings.title: Pair sizes for activity
settings.body: Use a smaller band for sport (less pull on the tabs) and a larger band for sleep (wider airflow). Many wearers keep both bedside.

settings.title: Give it 3–4 nights
settings.body: Your body adapts to nasal breathing over a few nights. The first night feels different — by night four it just feels right.

settings.title: Goggles & glasses
settings.body: Aira fits comfortably under most sunglasses without changing size. For swim goggles or face-plates, size down a notch.

settings.heading: Once you've got the basics.
settings.lead: A few things experienced wearers do differently. Worth a try once you're comfortable with the routine.

settings.question: Will it stay on if I move around in my sleep?
settings.answer: Yes — Aira was originally engineered for high-impact sport, so a side-sleeper is a low bar. The most common cause of slippage is missed prep: oily skin or skipping the 10-second press. Get those right and most wearers get a full 8 hours.

settings.question: How many tabs do I get per kit?
settings.answer: Your Aira Kit ships with the four bands plus 30 pairs of tabs — a full month of nightly wear. Refill packs come in 30, 60, or 90 pairs.

settings.question: Can I reuse a tab if I take it off mid-night?
settings.answer: Once a tab leaves the skin its adhesive picks up oils and lint, which weakens the next stick. You can re-press a tab the same night if it lifts, but each pair is designed for a single use.

settings.question: Is there anything I shouldn't do before applying?
settings.answer: Skip moisturisers, oils, and serums on the bridge of the nose for at least 5 minutes before. Apply Aira first, then your skincare around it.

settings.heading: Three steps. One better night.
settings.lead: Every Aira Kit ships with all four band sizes and 30 pairs of tabs — everything you need for your first month.
